title: weinmannia elliptica var. trichocarpa
intro: Weinmannia elliptica var. trichocarpa, also known as the mountain tōtara, is a plant species native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Māori medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and bark are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, mountain tōtara is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
